evals.name: Multiparty Agreement Pattern
evals.prompt: How do I implement a 'Multiple Party Agreement' pattern using Daml 3.4? Explain step-by-step and provide a template example.
evals.expected_output: Pending contract wrapper for the Agreement contract with multiple signatories logic.

assertions.text: Mentions the use of a 'Pending' contract wrapper.
Includes a choice that updates the list of signatories.
Atomic creation of the final agreement when all signatures are collected.

evals.name: JSON API V2 Query
evals.prompt: How can I list active contracts using TypeScript with the Canton JSON Ledger API V2? Provide a cURL example and a TS code snippet.
evals.expected_output: Usage of /v2/query endpoint and TypeScript codegen for ACS access.

assertions.text: Mentions dpm codegen-js for TypeScript bindings.
Provides a functioning TypeScript snippet using ledger context.

evals.name: Daml Script Reference
evals.prompt: How do I catch an authority error in Daml Script? Explain using submitMustFail or similar methods.
evals.expected_output: Usage of submitMustFail or DA.Exception handling in Daml Script.

assertions.text: Explains the use of submitMustFail for negative testing.
Mentions DA.Exception for catching logical errors.

evals.name: DPM Lifecycle
evals.prompt: Write the sequence of command line commands required to start a new Daml project and create a .dar file.
evals.expected_output: Sequences: dpm init -> dpm build -> DAR file creation.

assertions.text: Commands dpm init and dpm build are listed in order.
Mentions the production of a .dar file.
